title: Build a Compound Modal Component
prompt: Build a `Modal` compound component with sub-components `Modal.Trigger`, `Modal.Content`, and `Modal.Close`. The trigger opens the modal; the close button (or clicking the backdrop) dismisses it. Internal state is shared via Context — the consumer should not manage `isOpen` themselves.
context: The Compound Component pattern uses React Context to share implicit state between a parent and its designated children. This keeps the API ergonomic: `<Modal><Modal.Trigger>Open</Modal.Trigger><Modal.Content>...</Modal.Content></Modal>` — no prop drilling or external state.
